title: Renaissance art was a weapon - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer presents an argument that Renaissance art served as a strategic tool or weapon during the Renaissance period. The piece discusses the instrumental use of art in that era. The content is delivered as a podcast episode on Dwarkesh Patel's channel.
writeup: Historian Ada Palmer argues on the Dwarkesh Patel podcast that Renaissance art functioned as a strategic weapon rather than mere decoration. The conversation examines how art was put to instrumental use during the Renaissance period. The takeaway: aesthetic production in that era carried political and competitive purpose.
